mnemonics: PALS: Protocols, Addressing, Layering, Switching - The four core concepts of a systems approach.
keyPoints: A systems approach views the network as a whole, focusing on how components like protocols and algorithms interact.
Key design principles include layering, encapsulation, and the end-to-end argument.
Performance metrics like bandwidth, latency, and throughput are critical for understanding network behavior.
commonMistakes: Confusing bandwidth (capacity) with throughput (actual data transfer rate).
Thinking layers in the protocol stack are completely independent; they are designed to work together.
